entry.title: Combined summary - zkPoH: Zero-Knowledge Proof-of-Hodl
entry.summary: The concept of zkPoH (Zero-Knowledge Proof of Hodl) is introduced as an innovative system designed to demonstrate control over a specific amount of Bitcoin, specifically at least 1 BTC, without exposing sensitive information such as the UTXOs, addresses, transaction history, or private keys. The prototype operates by creating an off-chain snapshot of Bitcoin UTXOs which is then organized into a Merkle tree, establishing the Merkle root as a public commitment. The prover selects up to four UTXOs from this snapshot and uses Rust to generate witness inputs for the Noir program. The Noir circuit verifies that each selected UTXO belongs to the committed snapshot, validates the Merkle paths, and confirms that the cumulative value of the UTXOs meets the minimum requirement of 100,000,000 satoshis. Following the validation process, the only information disclosed to the verifier is that the prover holds at least 1 BTC in relation to the snapshot used, thereby maintaining privacy and anonymity. The current system utilizes Blake2s for hashing within the Merkle tree and requires external Bitcoin ownership verification through signed WIF proofs. However, there are considerations for future enhancements such as incorporating ownership verification directly into the circuit, introducing support for Schnorr/Taproot, and implementing other features like snapshot epochs, nullifiers, Utreexo commitments, and variable thresholds. This proof-of-concept serves not only as a technical experiment but also as an educational tool, illustrating potential advancements in privacy-preserving technologies in the Bitcoin ecosystem. Although it is not yet ready for production use, it paves the way for future development in private proof-of-hold systems. The ongoing work and further details can be accessed through the project's repository on GitHub, available at [github.com/fabohax/zkPoH](https://github.com/fabohax/zkPoH). This initiative highlights a significant step forward in leveraging zero-knowledge proofs to enhance security and privacy within Bitcoin transactions.
